pattern: The merged tree's diameter is determined by the diameters of the two original trees and the new edge — connect the optimal centers to minimize the longest path.
hints: Compute the diameter d1 and d2 of each tree using two BFS/DFS passes (first BFS from any node finds one endpoint; second BFS from that endpoint gives the diameter).
When you add one edge between the two trees, the worst-case path that crosses the edge is ceil(d1/2) + 1 + ceil(d2/2), because you can root each tree at its center to minimize its radius.
The answer is the maximum of d1, d2, and ceil(d1/2) + 1 + ceil(d2/2), since the final diameter is whichever of these three is largest.

edge_inputs.description: both trees are single nodes — merged diameter is 1
first tree is a path of 4 nodes, second is single node
long chain tree vs single node — diameter determined by the chain
star graph — diameter 2 — merging two stars checks radius calculation

stress.description: random trees built by connecting each new node to a random existing one
